content.text:
<user_query>
Review the provided implementation plan for a replacement sweep command + dispatcher + deploy backend scripts. Identify remaining gaps and risks before execution. Focus on:
- Objective alignment and scope completeness.
- High-risk execution issues: helper-mode/dry-run correctness, quoting robustness (shell/sed/paths), patching correctness for combo-spec injection, cancellation semantics, status/reporting.
- Migration compatibility with existing `sweep-fuyao` / deploy command behavior and existing user workflows.
- Security/operational risks: SSH/remote worktree reuse, run-id collisions, process cleanup, partial failures, resumability.
- Suggest concrete missing requirements and acceptance criteria.

Given only this plan, include specific recommended validations or invariants per script stage (dispatcher, deploy_for_you.sh, command wrapper).
Do not propose code; provide a structured gap checklist prioritized by severity.
</user_query>

<git_status>
This is the git status at the start of the conversation. Note that this status is a snapshot in time, and will not update during the conversation.

<agent_transcripts>
Agent transcripts (past chats) live in /Users/HanHu/.cursor/projects/Users-HanHu-software-motion-rl/agent-transcripts. They have names like <uuid>.jsonl, cite them to the user as [<title for chat <=6 words>](<uuid excluding .jsonl>). NEVER cite subagent transcripts/IDs; you can only cite parent uuids. Don't discuss the folder structure.
</agent_transcripts>

<agent_skills>
When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge. To use a skill, read the skill file at the provided absolute path using the ReadFile tool, then follow the instructions within. When a skill is relevant, read and follow it IMMEDIATELY as your first action. NEVER just announce or mention a skill without actually reading and following it. Only use skills listed below.

<available_skills description="Skills the agent can use. Use the ReadFile tool with the provided absolute path to fetch full contents.">
<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/checkout-for-huh8/SKILL.md">Derive and checkout huh8-prefixed git branches while preserving already-prefixed branches, and prompt for explicit suffix when creating a new branch from a huh8 source branch. Use when the user asks to create, rename, or checkout branches with huh8 naming or company-ID branch conventions.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/commit-tag-prefix/SKILL.md">Prepends git commit messages with an angle-bracketed type tag and enforces a consistent format. Use when creating commit messages, preparing git commit text, or when the user asks to commit staged changes.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/deploy-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic Fuyao deployment flow with strict validation, branch sync, and confirmation.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/hp-sweep-orchestrator/SKILL.md">Orchestrates hyperparameter sweep training jobs on Fuyao. Use when the user mentions hyperparameter sweep, parameter search, training sweep, sweep jobs, or wants to deploy multiple training runs with different config values.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/motion-rl-isaacgym-exec/SKILL.md">Executes shell commands inside the isaacgym container for the Motion RL workspace. Use when the user mentions Motion RL, motion_rl, isaacgym-motion_rl workspace, or asks to run commands in that workspace.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/plotjuggler-udp-fuzzy/SKILL.md">Generates and updates PlotJuggler layout XML and streaming XML using UDP JSON signals. Use when the user mentions PlotJuggler, layout files, streaming config, UDP telemetry, fuzzy signal names, or wants fuzzy-to-exact signal mapping.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/policy-lineage-tracker/SKILL.md">Interactive conversational agent for policy lineage experiment tracking. Parses natural-language requests, gathers missing context through guided questions, and calls the tracker CLI to record nodes, export mermaid diagrams, and populate lineage tables. Use when the user mentions policy lineage, experiment tracking, mutations, runs, tasks, tracker nodes, exporting lineage graphs, or describes training results.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/reward-design-analyzer/SKILL.md">Analyzes RL reward design for a registered task by tracing config inheritance, categorizing all reward terms, ranking importance, identifying external disturbances, and producing a Markdown report with tuning suggestions. Use when the user asks to analyze rewards, understand reward design, trace reward configs, tune rewards, or wants a reward breakdown for a task in the motion_rl / humanoid-gym codebase.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sweep-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic orchestration of Fuyao hyperparameter sweeps with explicit preview and verification.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sync-after-skill-create/SKILL.md">Legacy fallback for toolbox sync guidance. Prefer the personal rule `~/.cursor/rules/sync-toolbox-after-toolbox-edits.mdc` for primary behavior when editing toolbox assets.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-rule/SKILL.md">Create Cursor rules for persistent AI guidance. Use when you want to create a rule, add coding standards, set up project conventions, configure file-specific patterns, create RULE.md files, or asks about .cursor/rules/ or AGENTS.md.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-skill/SKILL.md">Guides users through creating effective Agent Skills for Cursor. Use when you want to create, write, or author a new skill, or asks about skill structure, best practices, or SKILL.md format.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/update-cursor-settings/SKILL.md">Modify Cursor/VSCode user settings in settings.json. Use when you want to change editor settings, preferences, configuration, themes, font size, tab size, format on save, auto save, keybindings, or any settings.json values.</agent_skill>
</available_skills>
</agent_skills>

Decision before execution: **Iterate** (plan is not yet safe to run end-to-end).

## Structured gap checklist (severity-ordered)

### High

1) `deploy_for_you.sh` (or replacement backend) must implement the `--helper-mode` API contract exactly as used by the dispatcher.
- Current parallel dispatcher invokes `--helper-mode deploy|status|log|cancel` and expects JSON outputs (`--receipt-out`, `--status-out`, `--log-out`, `--cancel-out`) when calling deploy script.
- Current deploy script (`deploy_fuyao.sh`) has no `--helper-mode` parser and exits on unknown option, so integration will fail immediately at runtime.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:769-789`, `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:882-900`, `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:1001-1030`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/deploy_fuyao.sh:145-180`

2) Dispatcher hard-codes backend script selection and cannot safely use a new backend without editing script internals.
- `orchestrator_parallel.sh` sets `DEPLOY_SCRIPT` to a fixed path and does not read a user-specified deploy backend from payload.
- This means a “new deploy backend” plan is blocked unless this hardcoded path is changed, which is a direct compatibility risk.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:5`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator.sh:5` (same pattern for legacy path to deploy script)

3) Patching semantics and `hp_class_map` support are currently incomplete for custom sweep specs.
- Both dispatchers patch via simple regex replace and fail if a key is not already present (`n == 0` case), so custom keys or class-embedded keys break sweeps.
- Existing user workflow/docs advertise `hp_class_map` support, and `hp_patcher.py` already provides class-aware replacement+injection behavior that is not used in dispatcher patching.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:256-282`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator.sh:259-286`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/commands/sweep-fuyao.md:32,142`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/hp_patcher.py:85-87`, `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/hp_patcher.py:234-257`

4) End-to-end status/reporting compatibility mismatch between new parallel flow and documented verify workflow.
- Dispatcher writes `.submit.log`, `.start_validation.log`, `.cancel.log`, plus JSON artifacts.
- `verify_fuyao_jobs.sh` still scans `logs/*.dispatch.log` for job names, so post-sweep verification would fail to resolve jobs in this runtime shape.
- This is a critical operational gap for user feedback and triage.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:731-770`, `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:1320-1366` (artifact set)
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh:32-35,53-63,184-191`

5) Migration gap in user-facing cancellation command contract (`--cancel-sweep`) vs script interface.
- Command contract references `--cancel-sweep` for test-run cleanup, but the orchestrator entry points only accept payload/interactive modes.
- This creates a documented-but-unimplemented path and breaks recovery/cleanup workflow consistency.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/commands/sweep-fuyao.md:93-99`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator.sh:1088-1107`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:1088-1107`

6) Current parallel orchestration auto-cancels every successfully submitted live job in each cycle (submit -> start_validate -> cancel) and marks success only if cancel confirmed.
- If the objective is “run the sweep and keep training,” this design will produce immediate teardown behavior and false-negative completion perception unless explicitly documented as a test-only pattern.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:837-855`, `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:1286-1318`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:974-1031`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:1318-1323`

### Medium

7) Run-id collisions are possible under concurrent launches.
- Sweep IDs are second-resolution timestamps with no uniqueness guard.
- Two parallel invocations in the same second can collide and overwrite run artifacts or remote worktree roots.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator.sh:527-531`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:1202-1204`

8) Partial failure recovery / resumability is weak.
- Once a cycle stops early (or exits on errors), there is no explicit replay command path in code, even though docs/workflow mention cancellation and rerun patterns.
- You get failure summaries, but not deterministic resume semantics by persisted artifact state.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:1289-1336`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/commands/sweep-fuyao.md:94-101`, `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/commands/sweep-fuyao.md:125-130`

9) Cleanup robustness on interruption is incomplete.
- The parallel worker model waits on per-cycle jobs, but there is no global trap/cleanup handler for premature termination, so remote worktrees and local background state can leak on abrupt stops.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:1042-1055`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:1284-1285` (cleanup occurs inside normal path only)

10) Quoting/host security posture has known weak points.
- Remote deploy script builds command strings and executes through `ssh` with interpolated pieces; deployment defaults include host-key checking disabled in remote sync flow.
- This is not strictly a functional blocker, but it is a nontrivial operational risk in hardened environments.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/deploy_fuyao.sh:358-372`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/deploy_fuyao.sh:291`

### Low

11) Defaults/aliases drift across command docs vs scripts.
- Example: queue defaults and SSH aliases are not fully aligned in existing docs/scripts (`remote.kernel.fuyo` vs `Huh8.remote_kernel.fuyao`, queue variants with/without `-share` in different files), which can confuse users and lead to wrong-site behavior if not normalized.
- References:
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/commands/deploy-fuyao.md:15-21,24-29,25-31,14?`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/commands/sweep-fuyao.md:23-28`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/deploy_fuyao.sh:9-20`, `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/orchestrator_parallel.sh:10-14`
  - `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh:9-10,24-25`

## Missing requirements + acceptance criteria (recommended, stage-wise)

### Dispatcher stage
- Add explicit payload contract validation for `helper-mode` compatibility fields (`confirm_live_submit`, `confirm_live_cancel`, `receipt/status/log/cancel` paths, marker configs, timeout knobs), fail-fast with clear messages on missing/invalid values.
- Preserve and propagate `hp_class_map` from payload, then patch through class-aware logic or explicit `hp_patcher.py` flow; error if unknown key appears.
- Ensure sweep id uniqueness and detect pre-existing `run_root`/remote worktree collisions before submission.
- Keep run artifact shape stable and document expected files (`run_manifest`, per-combo payload/log/status/artifacts).
- Acceptance check: one dry-run payload with two parameters and one unknown hyper key returns a deterministic pre-flight failure that points to `hp_class_map` remediation, with no remote mutation.

### `deploy_for_you.sh` stage
- Implement full `--helper-mode` modes:
  - `deploy`: writes machine-readable `receipt.json` with fields consumed by orchestrator (`success`, `submitted`, `job_name`, `job_id`, timestamps).
  - `status`: writes `status.json` (`is_running`, `is_terminal`, `status`, `code`, `error` fields).
  - `log`: writes marker results and optional failure markers (`has_training_marker`, `has_failure_marker`, marker arrays).
  - `cancel`: writes cancel-termination evidence (`terminal_state_confirmed`, `cancel_rc`, reason).
- Implement idempotent dry-run behavior across all modes: no remote side effects.
- Replace shell-string command composition with safer argument passing (arrays or JSON payload path) and validate required args before SSH.
- Acceptance check: orchestrator can call all four modes and consume outputs without parser failures.

### Command wrapper stage (`/sweep-fuyao` equivalent flow)
- Keep required-input UX/fuzzy task/branch behavior and preview contract from existing command docs and skill.
- Preserve dry-run semantics by requiring no branch push/remote actions and clearly emitting generated artifact paths/commands.
- Add explicit confirmation gating for live submit and live cancel; pass that intent through to dispatcher (`confirm_live_submit`, `confirm_live_cancel`).
- Normalize defaults with docs and existing command behavior (or explicitly document changed defaults/behavior).
- Ensure cancel/recovery UX is actually implemented in scripts; if `--cancel-sweep` remains documented, provide a tested command path.
- Acceptance check: a user-visible sweep request can complete preview, confirm, submit, verify (or fail-fast with actionable error), and cancellation request executes reliably.

Open question to finalize before execution:
- Confirm whether current cycle design (submit → validate → **immediate cancel**) is intentional test-only behavior, or should production sweeps keep jobs alive after validation; this decision changes both dispatcher logic and status interpretation.
